recap: Erling Haaland's two goals in the final 11 minutes completed Norway's stunning upset of Brazil, who had squandered an early penalty when Bruno Guimarães's spot kick was saved and dominated possession for large spells without converting their chances. Goalkeeper Orjan Nyland made several crucial stops to keep Norway in the contest before Haaland's decisive header and clinical finish secured a 2-1 victory that sent the Scandinavians to their first World Cup quarterfinal in 28 years. Neymar's stoppage-time penalty provided only a consolation goal as Brazil crashed out in the round of 16.
